desc-default: This section gathers general computer science concepts, handy for having course notes quickly at hand.
panel-nav1-intro: Basic network concepts, to understand how computers manage to communicate with each other.
item1-1-desc: The famous theoretical communication model that governs communication between machines.
panel-nav2-intro: The computer's components and how they interact.
panel-nav3-intro: A reminder of the good habits that keep an information system secure.
item3-1-desc: Three… Two… One… Zero! … And one? … Zero?
item3-2-desc: What would be the right length? How often should they be changed? What should be avoided?
panel-nav4-intro: To understand the theory hidden behind our computers.
item4-1-desc: It is the basis for building logic gates, which, applied to electronics, give rise to processors.
item4-2-desc: These concepts help highlight whether a hash function is reversible or not
item4-3-desc: Used to predict and measure, this branch of mathematics is especially relevant in the age of Big Data and artificial intelligence
